subject: Literature in English
source: Liberia Ministry of Education National Curriculum

objectives: Discuss literary devices in a given drama
Identify various themes in a drama with textual evidence
Identify the various characters and their roles
Identify the major and minor conflicts in the drama
content: Main issues running through the drama
Kinds of drama: Tragedy, Comedy, Tragic-comedy
activities: Class discussion on figurative expressions (Metaphor, Simile, Personification)
Assign learners to read selected Shakespearean drama
Group learners to discuss main issues running through the drama
Learners to dramatize the play
Assign learners to write their own play
primary_texts: A Midsummer Night's Dream by William Shakespeare
Let Me Die Alone by John K. Kargbo
A Government Driver On His Retirement by Onu Chibuike
supplementary_resources: Listen to the Story Teller — A Critical Thinking Liberian Anthology (T. Michael Weah & Llord Aidoo)

objectives: Interpret Shakespearean drama
Distinguish various types of drama
Outline the traits and roles of characters in the drama
Utilize figurative expressions in a given work of arts
content: The traits and roles of characters in a drama
activities: Discuss types of drama, traits and roles of characters in the drama
Assign learners to read selected Shakespearean drama
Assign learners to identify and discuss figurative expressions as used in the drama
Group learners to write their own drama
primary_texts: A Midsummer Night's Dream by William Shakespeare
The Lion and the Jewel by Wole Soyinka
Black Woman by L.S. Senghor
Bats by D.H. Lawrence

objectives: Sequence drama by scenes and acts
Explain Drama Exposition
Distinguish the different types of drama
Develop drama writing skills
Relate events in a drama to real life situations
content: Literary devices: Exposition, Plot, Conflict, Climax, Resolution, Tragic Hero, Tragic Flaw, Protagonist, Antagonist, Irony, Dialogue
activities: Assign learners to read selected African drama
Divide learners into groups to brainstorm on the similarities and differences of the different types of drama
Learners discuss drama exposition and critically answer questions on selected African drama
Assign learners to identify and discuss Literary devices
Divide learners in pairs to write their own plays and act out the best play in class
primary_texts: The Lion and the Jewel by Wole Soyinka
Second Class Citizen by Buchi Emecheta
The Rock by Darlington Johnson
The Definition of Love by Andrew Marvell
supplementary_resources: Listen to the Story Teller — A Critical Thinking Liberian Anthology (T. Michael Weah & Llord Aidoo)

objectives: Interpret different types of Prose
Appreciate Prose as a work of art
Analyze Prose using Literary devices
content: Types of Prose: Fictional Prose, Non-fictional Prose, Heroic Prose
activities: Group learners to discuss and interpret different types of prose
Assign learners to read selected African Prose and present report
Learners discuss and analyze Prose using figurative expression
primary_texts: Second Class Citizen by Buchi Emecheta
The Invisible Man by Ralph Ellison
Caged Bird by Maya Angelou
The Song Of Women Of My Land by Oumar Farouk Sesay
supplementary_resources: The Spot by T. Michael Weah

objectives: Outline subject matter, conflict, setting, moral, cultural values and implementations in selected African Prose
Utilize figurative expressions
Summarize African Prose in the form of book reports
Analyze different kinds of writings
Make inferences of the point of view of a novel
content: Point of view from which a Prose is written
activities: Guide learners to read African Prose
Discuss the use of figurative expressions
Discuss the format of a book report; have learners write book report
Learners write Prose using figures of speech to create images
Discuss the writing process of different kinds of writings; have learners prepare essay and descriptions
Discuss the point of view from which a Prose is written
Write a book report
primary_texts: Unexpected Joy at Dawn by Alex Agyei-Agyiri
Fences by August Wilson
The Leader and the Lead by Nugi Osundare
Journey of the Magi by T.S. Eliot
supplementary_resources: The Rain and the Night by Wilton Sankawulo

objectives: Analyze African Drama and Prose based on theme, mood, setting and literary devices
Discuss the kinds of Drama and Prose
content: Elements of drama and prose: Theme, Setting, Mood, Plot, Conflict, Climax, Literary devices
activities: Assign learners to read selected Shakespearean drama
Divide learners into groups to discuss the elements of drama and prose
Learners work in groups to analyze given drama and prose
primary_texts: Look Back In Anger by John Osborne
Wuthering Heights by Emily Bronte
The Grieved Lands by Agostinho Neto
Do Not Go Gentle Into That Good Night by Dylan Thomas
Felled-1879-Binsey Poplars by G.M. Hopkins

objectives: Identify elements of non-African prose
Analyze roles of characters in selected non-African prose
Prepare a summary report of selected non-African prose using approved formats
Utilize figurative expressions in given texts
content: Non-African prose: Themes, Style, Content, Plot, Conflict
Book report format: Title, Author/Genre, Published date, Summary (setting, theme), Characters, Purpose, Plot
activities: Learners read selected non-African prose and discuss themes, style, plot, content, and conflict
Assign learners to outline and discuss figurative expressions used in non-African prose
Learners adopt and use sample format to write book report of selected non-African prose
primary_texts: Native Son by Richard Wright
She Stoops To Conquer by Oliver Goldsmith
Vanity by Birago Diop
Birches by Robert Frost
supplementary_resources: The Village Son by Dr. E. Kandakai

objectives: Analyze African Prose in relation to the writer's point of view
Determine textual evidence in an African prose
Identify literary devices used in African Prose
Formulate and defend personal opinions on selected African Prose
Discuss sexuality issues based on the selected African Prose text
content: Cultural background of texts noting important ideas and major substance of the storyline
Themes in a prose: grief, social class, land issue, love, women's role in society, family loyalty, violence
activities: Assign learners to read selected African prose and formulate/defend personal opinions
Learners discuss author's background and style, various themes, the story's main line, and cultural relevance
Discuss Sexuality issues emanating from the African Prose
Learners compose own short stories
primary_texts: Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
Faceless by Amma Darko
Piano And Drum by Gabriel Okara
Crossing The Bar by Alfred Tennyson
Africa's Plea by Roland T. Dempster
The Human But by Roland T. Dempster
A Song Out of Midnight by Roland T. Dempster

objectives: Differentiate the types of poetry and their forms
Appreciate poetry using literary devices and imageries
Apply poetic devices in poetry
content: Types of Poetry: Narrative, Lyric, Dramatic, Pastoral, Didactic, Satiric, Sonnet, Elegy, Ballad
Literary devices: Foreshadowing, Suspense, Flashback, Symbolism
activities: Assign learners to read and appreciate selected poems
Group learners to review and discuss poetic devices
Divide learners into groups to discuss the types of poems; characteristics of Shakespearean and Petrarchan sonnets
Assign learners project to write short poem
primary_texts: The Panic of Growing Older by Lenre Peters
Shall I Compare Thee to a Summer's Day — Shakespearean Sonnet
The Dining Table by Gbanabom Hallowell
A Government Driver on His Retirement (African)
The Song of the Women of My Land
Caged Bird (Non-African)
Journey of the Magi
Bat by David H. Lawrence (Non-African)

objectives: Discuss non-African drama and elements
Utilize figurative language in Non-African Drama
Analyze Non-African drama by scenes and acts
Appreciate cultural values, practices, and implications in non-African drama
content: Exposition (plot, conflict, climax, resolution), theme, tragedy, comedy
Literary Devices: Irony, Flashback, Foreshadowing, Dialogue, Tragic Hero
activities: Assign learners to read and summarize selected Non-African drama
Learners discuss the plot diagram, theme, tragedy, comedy, and tragic-comedy in non-African drama
Learners discuss and outline literary devices used by writers in Non-African drama
Place learners in groups to discuss the implications of cultural values and practices in non-African drama
Learners write and role play drama in groups
primary_texts: The Castle Of Otranto by Horace Walpole
The Proud King by William Morris
Native Son by Richard Wright

objectives: Discuss the exposition, themes, and characters of selected African drama
Explain the different types of drama
Compare and contrast dramatic forms from different cultures
content: Drama is written in acts and scenes
Culture, values, norms, and their significance to society
Main issues and ideas running through the drama
activities: Assign learners to read selected African Drama and share opinions with the class
Organize learners to outline and discuss the use of literary terminologies in drama writing
Demonstrate acts and scenes of drama by depicting characteristics of the characters
Learners analyze themes and characters from selected dramas making connections to self, text and the world
primary_texts: Harvest of Corruption by Frank Ogodo Ogbeche
The Blood of a Stranger by Dele Charley
Ambush by Gbemisola Adeoti
The School Boy by William Blake
Let Me Die Alone by John K. Kargbo
The Lion and the Jewel by Wole Soyinka

topics.topic: African and Non-African Prose, Poetry and Drama (Review)
objectives: Examine issues of marriage and faithfulness in the story as well as the treatment of widows
Analyze the roles of major and minor characters in a story
Describe the techniques used by poets to convey feelings and emotions
Depict character roles using graphic organizers
content: African Prose Themes: Cultural beliefs, Maltreatment, Loneliness, Widowhood, Hostility, Death
Literary devices: Foreshadowing, Suspense, Flashback, Symbolism
activities: Assign learners to read selected Non-African prose, drama and poetry
Class discussion on various themes in selected prose
Divide learners into groups to discuss and develop graphic organizers to depict character roles
Pair learners to write short stories, plays and poems
primary_texts: Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
She Stoops to Conquer by Oliver Goldsmith
Native Son by Richard Wright
The Blood of a Stranger by Dele Charley
Invisible Man by Ralph Ellison
Let Me Die Alone by John K. Kargbo
Piano and Drums by Gabriel Okara
The Panic of Growing Older by Lenre Peters
Crossing the Bar by Alfred Tennyson
Shall I Compare Thee to a Summer's Day — Shakespearean Sonnet
A Government Driver on His Retirement
The Song of the Women of My Land
Caged Bird
Journey of the Magi
Let Me Live My Life by Roland T. Dempster
Citizen ID Card by Lekpele M. Nyamalon

topics.topic: Review African Poems and Figurative Expressions
objectives: Analyze a poem based on theme, form, tone, mood, literary devices, cultural and societal relevance
Create short poems using different themes
Utilize figurative expressions in poems
content: African Poems: Subject-matter, Tone, Atmosphere, Mood, Literary devices, Relevance to society, Form
activities: Assign learners to review and analyze elements of selected African poems
Review figurative expressions and discuss literary devices and their cultural and societal relevance
Learners analyze and write poems using different themes and figurative expressions
primary_texts: The Panic of Growing Older by Lenre Peters
Vanity by Birago Diop
The Anvil and the Hammer by Kofi Awoonor
Piano and Drums by Gabriel Okara
The Dining Table by Gbanabom Hallowell
Ambush by Gbemisola Adeoti
Black Woman by Leopold Sedar Senghor
A Government Driver on His Retirement by Onu Chibuike

topics.topic: African Prose, Poetry and Literary Devices
objectives: Summarize the selected novel in the form of a book report
Examine the main issues running through the story
Relate themes in novel to society or other situations
Analyze characters in a novel using graphic organizers
content: Non-African prose: Themes, Style, Conflict, Plot
Use of Literary Devices: Symbolism, Characterization, Imagery, Foreshadowing, Suspense, Flashback
activities: Assign learners to review and summarize selected novels
Class discussion on the various themes of the novel
Learners work in groups to outline and discuss literary devices
Assign learners to summarize the novel in the form of book reports and present to class
primary_texts: Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
Faceless by Amma Darko
The Leader and the Lead by Niyi Osundare
The Grieved Lands by Agostinho Neto
The Song of the Women of My Land by Oumar Farouk Sesay
Raider of the Treasure Trove by Lade Wosonu
Wuthering Heights by Emily Bronte
The Mystic Reformation of Gondolia by Roland T. Dempster

topics.topic: Review of Non-African Drama, Poetry and Literary Devices
objectives: Discuss drama as a representation of life, style, culture, values, and norms
Review literary devices
Distinguish the types of drama, their cultural and contemporary relevance
Explain the use of flashback and imagery in non-African Drama
content: Reviewing Literary devices: Comedy, Tragedy, Tragic-Comedy, Melodrama
activities: Group learners to list and discuss literary devices used in non-African drama
Assign learners to review the use of flashback, imagery, suspense and dictions in selected non-African drama
Assign learners to review and write a report on selected Non-African drama
primary_texts: Othello by Shakespeare
She Stoops to Conquer by Oliver Goldsmith
A Raisin in the Sun by Lorraine Hansberry
A Midsummer Night's Dream by Shakespeare
Felled-1879-Binsey Poplars by G.M. Hopkins
Do Not Go Gentle into the Good Night by Dylan Thomas

topics.topic: Prose, Poetry, Drama and Literary Devices
objectives: Compare and contrast prose, poetry, and drama as part of literary work
Summarize the themes and lessons learned in selected prose, poetry and drama
Compose prose, poetry, and drama using literary devices
content: Literary Devices: Style, Content, Character roles and traits, Theme, Tone, Mood
activities: Assign learners in groups to compare and contrast prose, poetry, and drama and make presentation
Discuss and review literary devices derived from selected literary works
Assign learners to analyze selected literary works, summarize themes and explain lessons learned
Assign each learner a project to write short prose, poetry and drama
primary_texts: Faceless by Amma Darko
Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
Native Son by Richard Wright
A Raisin in the Sun by Lorraine Hansberry
Crossing the Bar by Lord Alfred Tennyson
I Thank God — A Birthday Poem by Roland T. Dempster

objectives: Answer examination questions observing content, expression, mechanics, and style
Apply knowledge gained from past examinations to respond effectively to future examinations
content: Answer questions from past WASSCE papers
Discuss past questions in prose, poetry, drama
Discuss various themes, characters, exposition, tone, mood
Review contents, expressions, mechanics, and styles
Drill for literary devices using examples
activities: Review past WASSCE examinations, especially on how to answer public test questions
Discuss literary devices
Review and discuss questions in prose, poetry, and drama
Review and discuss themes, characters, exposition, tone, and mood
Review and discuss different mechanics, diction, and styles used in writings
Review and discuss various literary devices with examples
primary_texts: Faceless by Amma Darko
Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
The Last Goodman by Patience Swift
She Stoops to Conquer by Oliver Goldsmith
Vanity by Birago Diop

topics.topic: More Review with Past Examination Papers, Prose, Poems, and Drama
objectives: Answer examination questions observing content, expression, mechanics, and style
Analyze past examination questions
Summarize any given poem, novel, and play
Utilize literary devices effectively
content: Reviewing past examination questions on selected Poems, Novels and Plays
Discuss past questions in Prose, Poetry, Drama
Discuss Themes, Characters, Exposition, Tone, Mood, and Plots
Drill for literary devices using examples
activities: Reviewing and discussing past WASSCE examinations questions on selected poems, novels, and plays
Survey of literary devices
Review and discuss various themes, characters, exposition, tone, mood and plots
Review and discuss the mechanics, diction, and style used in given work

Lonely Days by Bayo Adebowale
Native Son by Richard Wright
The Castle of Otranto by Horace Walpole
Othello by Shakespeare
